# Industrial Capacity Utilization Rate in the Third Quarter of 2024

2024-10-19 10:00  [Print](javascript:window.print())| [Large](javascript:;)| [Medium](javascript:;)| [Small](javascript:;)

In the third quarter of 2024, the industrial capacity utilization rate of industrial enterprises above the designated size nationwide was 75.1 percent, down by 0.5 percentage point from the same period of the previous year and up by 0.2 percentage point from the previous quarter.

 

 

In terms of sectors, in the third quarter of 2024, the capacity utilization rate of the mining industry was 74.6 percent; the capacity utilization rate of the manufacturing industry was 75.2 percent; the capacity utilization rate of production and supply of electricity, heat power, gas and water was 74.3 percent.

 

In terms of major industries, in the third quarter of 2024, the capacity utilization rate of the mining and washing of coal was 72.7 percent, that of the manufacture of foods was 70.7 percent, that of the textile industry was 77.9 percent, that of the manufacture of raw chemical materials and chemical products was 76.0 percent, that of the manufacture of non-metallic mineral products was 61.7 percent, that of the smelting and pressing of ferrous metals was 77.4 percent, that of the smelting and pressing of non-ferrous metals was 78.4 percent, that of the manufacture of general purpose machinery was 78.7 percent, that of the manufacture of special purpose machinery was 75.8 percent, that of manufacture of automobiles was 73.2 percent, that of the manufacture of electrical machinery and apparatus was 75.6 percent, and that of the manufacture of computers, communication equipment and other electronic equipment was 77.9 percent.

 

Industrial Capacity Utilization Rate in the Third Quarter of 2024

 

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  |  | First Three Quarters |
| Capacity Utilization Rate | Increase or Decrease Rate  (±%) Y/Y | Capacity Utilization Rate  (%) | Increase or Decrease Rate  (±%) Y/Y |
| Industrial Enterprises Above the Designated Size | 75.1 | -0.5 | 74.6 | -0.2 |
| Of which: Mining | 74.6 | -0.8 | 75.2 | 0.1 |
| Manufacturing | 75.2 | -0.6 | 74.8 | -0.2 |
| Production and supply of electricity, heat power, gas and water | 74.3 | 0.1 | 72.3 | -0.2 |
| Of which: Mining and washing of coal | 72.7 | -1.1 | 72.3 | -1.7 |
| Extraction of petroleum and natural gas | 90.2 | -1.0 | 91.3 | 0.2 |
| Manufacture of foods | 70.7 | -0.4 | 69.5 | -0.4 |
| Textile industry | 77.9 | 1.0 | 78.3 | 1.9 |
| Manufacture of raw chemical materials and chemical products | 76.0 | 1.4 | 76.3 | 1.5 |
| Manufacture of medicines | 73.8 | 1.1 | 75.1 | 0.3 |
| Manufacture of chemical fibers | 85.5 | 0.4 | 85.4 | 1.6 |
| Manufacture of non-metallic mineral products | 61.7 | -3.3 | 62.6 | -2.1 |
| Smelting and pressing of ferrous metals | 77.4 | -1.7 | 78.2 | -0.5 |
| Smelting and pressing of non-ferrous metals | 78.4 | -1.8 | 78.6 | -0.8 |
| Manufacture of general purpose machinery | 78.7 | 0.0 | 78.8 | -0.4 |
| Manufacture of special purpose machinery | 75.8 | -1.7 | 76.4 | -1.1 |
| Manufacture of automobiles | 73.2 | -2.4 | 70.3 | -3.4 |
| Manufacture of electrical machinery and apparatus | 75.6 |  |  |  |
| Manufacture of computers, communication equipment and other electronic equipment | 77.9 | 1.4 | 76.3 |  |

 

 

1. Explanatory Notes

 

Capacity utilization rate: refers to the ratio of actual output to production capacity (both measured by value).

 

The actual output of the enterprise refers to the total industrial output value of the enterprise in the reporting period; the production capacity of the enterprise refers to the product output that the enterprise can achieve and can maintain for a long time when the production equipment (machinery) maintains normal operation under the condition that the supply of labor, raw materials, fuel, transport, etc. is guaranteed during the reporting period.

 

2. Survey Method and Statistical Coverage

 

The survey involving about 110,000 industrial enterprises above the designated size includes a comprehensive survey of large and medium-sized enterprises and a sample survey of micro and small enterprises above the designated size.

 

Extrapolate aggregate data of micro and small enterprises based on sampling method were synthesized with the survey data of large and medium-sized enterprises to calculate the national industrial capacity utilization rate of industrial enterprises above the designated size.

 

The survey was conducted on a quarterly basis and the data acquired have not been seasonally adjusted.

 

3. Standard on Industrial Classification

 

The National Industrial Classification of All Economic Activities (GB/T4754-2017) was performed.
